reasoning: Weekly stock price movements are largely unpredictable and generally hover around a 50% probability, with a slight bias upward due to the equity risk premium. The statistical prediction from historical data gives a 55% chance of an increase based on linear trends, but a combined estimate of 44% when factoring in a seasonal drop during this week in the one prior year evaluated. The period in question is also shortened to just 4 trading days (due to Good Friday on April 3, 2026, meaning markets close Thursday, April 2). Without any major scheduled catalysts such as earnings (which are mid-April) or dividends (ex-div was February), there is no strong fundamental reason to deviate significantly from approximately 0.5. Giving slight weight to the 0.44 combined model estimate and recent choppiness near all-time highs, but balancing it with general market drift, I estimate 0.49.
